prompt: Construct a FleetReserve Operations Suite supporting offline vehicle fleet scheduling, e-ticket admissions, and auditable multi-role access for a local rental yard or dealership service center. The Leptos web UI runs in a browser on the facility network and presents a unified availability calendar that operators can switch between day/week views, filter by location and asset status, and create reservations for vehicles and related service bays in 15-minute increments within configurable business hours (default 7:00 AM–7:00 PM). When a user proposes a booking, the interface immediately explains conflicts in plain language (overlapping reservation, vehicle in-repair hold, expired insurance, or capacity exceeded) and suggests the nearest two alternative time slots and, when applicable, an alternate eligible vehicle or bay. Roles are explicit and task-oriented: Customers can sign in and view their upcoming reservations and tickets, Photographers can access only assignments tied to their jobs, Merchants/Store Staff can manage local fleet assets and reservations for their store, Platform Operations can manage cross-store calendars and exports, and Administrators can manage roles, permissions, and recovery actions. E-tickets are generated at reservation confirmation and can be displayed as a QR code plus a human-readable ticket number; a check-in screen supports fast scanning or manual entry, shows validity windows (for example, valid 03/27/2026 9:00 AM–12:00 PM), and blocks re-entry after one successful redemption while still allowing a supervised “undo” within 2 minutes for operator mistakes with a mandatory reason entry.The backend uses Axum to expose local REST-style APIs consumed by the Leptos frontend, with SQLite as the on-device system of record for vehicles, reservations, tickets, users, roles, and audit trails. Conflict detection must be strongly consistent under concurrency: reservation creation executes in a transaction with optimistic concurrency checks on the targeted vehicle/bay and time window, retrying up to 3 times before returning a deterministic conflict reason and alternatives computed from the same committed snapshot. Vehicle master data includes VIN, license plate, make/model/trim, photos uploaded from local files, assigned store, mileage (miles), fuel or battery percentage, upcoming maintenance/inspection/insurance expirations, and controlled status transitions (available, reserved, on-rent, in-repair, decommissioned) that require appropriate permission and are fully logged. Authentication is strictly offline: username plus password only, with passwords hashed using Argon2id and local password reset via admin-issued one-time recovery codes that expire in 30 minutes; sessions use signed tokens with a 12-hour idle timeout and CSRF protection for state-changing requests, plus standard XSS defenses through output encoding and a restrictive content policy. Security and compliance auditing is end-to-end: every create/update/delete, approval decision, permission change, export action, and ticket redemption writes a tamper-evident append-only log chain stored in SQLite with periodic hash anchors; sensitive fields (VIN, license plate, user identifiers) are masked by default in the UI and encrypted at rest using a locally managed key. File uploads are restricted to JPEG/PNG under 10 MB, validated by MIME sniffing and magic bytes, stripped of executable content, and fingerprinted to prevent duplicate storage. Backup and recovery are offline-first, producing an encrypted SQLite backup file to a user-selected local path, with restore limited to Administrators and recorded in the audit trail.
